 {"id":"bd-wph","title":"Create Benchmark Suite","description":"## Objective
Establish a comprehensive benchmark suite using criterion for measuring performance.

## Background
Before optimizing, we need reliable benchmarks to:
1. Establish baseline performance
2. Measure improvement from optimizations
3. Detect regressions in CI

## Benchmarks to Create

### Color Benchmarks
- [ ] Parse named color (cold cache)
- [ ] Parse named color (warm cache, after LRU implemented)
- [ ] Parse hex color
- [ ] Color downgrade (TrueColor -> Standard)

### Style Benchmarks
- [ ] Style::parse() simple
- [ ] Style::parse() complex
- [ ] Style::combine()
- [ ] Style::render_to_ansi()

### Text Benchmarks
- [ ] Text::render() short text
- [ ] Text::render() long text
- [ ] Text::wrap() narrow width
- [ ] Text::wrap() wide width
- [ ] Text::divide() with many spans

### Table Benchmarks
- [ ] Small table (3x3)
- [ ] Medium table (10x10)
- [ ] Large table (100x100)
- [ ] Table with styled cells
- [ ] Table width calculation only

### Cell Width Benchmarks
- [ ] cell_len() ASCII
- [ ] cell_len() mixed (ASCII + CJK)
- [ ] cell_len() emoji-heavy

### End-to-End Benchmarks
- [ ] Console::print() with markup
- [ ] Panel rendering
- [ ] Full page render (table + panel + text)

## Implementation
```rust
// benches/benchmarks.rs
use criterion::{criterion_group, criterion_main, Criterion};

fn color_parsing(c: &mut Criterion) {
    c.bench_function("parse_named_color", |b| {
        b.iter(|| Color::parse("red"))
    });
}

criterion_group!(benches, color_parsing);
criterion_main!(benches);
```

## Files to Create
- benches/benchmarks.rs
- Cargo.toml (add criterion dev-dependency)","acceptance_criteria":"Benchmark suite created. All major code paths covered. Results documented.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:24:55.471565885Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T07:08:32.259566095Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T07:08:32.259566095Z","close_reason":"Benchmark suite already implemented in benches/render_bench.rs. Covers: color parsing (named, hex, rgb, indexed), style parsing (simple/complex), text render/wrap, cell_len (ASCII/CJK/emoji/mixed), table render (3x3, 10x5), panel render. Uses criterion 0.8. Core suite complete.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-wph","depends_on_id":"bd-yz6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:11Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-x25","title":"Validate Progress Components (ProgressBar, Spinner)","description":"Validate Progress components against RICH_SPEC.md Section 16 (if exists) or Python Rich source.

COMPONENTS TO VALIDATE:
1. ProgressBar struct and rendering
2. BarStyle enum and customization
3. Spinner struct and animation frames

VALIDATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] ProgressBar width calculation matches spec
- [ ] ProgressBar percentage display formatting
- [ ] ProgressBar fill characters (complete vs incomplete)
- [ ] BarStyle custom character support
- [ ] Spinner frame cycling logic
- [ ] Spinner Unicode character sets
- [ ] Color/style support for all components

IMPLEMENTATION NOTES:
- Progress components render to Segments
- Width should respect console width constraints
- Spinner should be stateless (frame index passed in)

FILES TO EXAMINE:
- src/renderables/progress.rs
- Python rich/progress_bar.py for reference
- Python rich/spinner.py for reference","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:11:00.317920614Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T18:25:10.265841141Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T18:25:10.265841141Z","close_reason":"## Validation Complete - Progress Components

### Key Finding: Phase 2+ Feature Implemented Early

**RICH_SPEC.md explicitly states**: "Progress bars | Separate concern, Phase 2+"

This means Progress components are **NOT in Phase 1 scope** and have no formal spec to validate against. The implementation is ahead of schedule.

---

### ProgressBar Implementation ✅ WELL-DESIGNED

**File**: src/renderables/progress.rs (lines 197-612)

| Feature | Status | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| Width calculation | ✅ | Respects available space |
| Percentage display | ✅ | show_percentage option |
| ETA calculation | ✅ | Based on elapsed time |
| Speed (items/sec) | ✅ | show_speed option |
| Elapsed time | ✅ | show_elapsed option |
| Description | ✅ | Optional task name |
| Finished message | ✅ | Custom completion text |
| Brackets | ✅ | Optional [ ] around bar |
| Style support | ✅ | Separate styles for completed/remaining/pulse |

**BarStyle enum** (5 variants):
- Ascii: # and -
- Block: █ and ░ (default)
- Line: ━ and ─
- Dots: ● and ○
- Gradient: █ and ░ with ▓ pulse

---

### Spinner Implementation ✅ WELL-DESIGNED

**File**: src/renderables/progress.rs (lines 66-195)

| Feature | Status | Notes |
|---------|--------|-------|
| Frame cycling | ✅ | next_frame() advances |
| Stateless query | ✅ | current_frame() doesn't advance |
| Style support | ✅ | Applied to rendered segment |
| Custom frames | ✅ | custom() constructor |

**Preset spinners** (7 variants):
- dots: ⠋⠙⠹⠸⠼⠴⠦⠧⠇⠏
- line: ⎺⎻⎼⎽⎼⎻
- simple: |/-\
- bounce: ⠁⠂⠄⠂
- growing: ⣾⣽⣻⢿⡿⣟⣯⣷
- moon: 🌑🌒🌓🌔🌕🌖🌗🌘
- clock: 🕐🕑🕒🕓🕔🕕🕖🕗🕘🕙🕚🕛

---

### Unit Tests ✅ COMPREHENSIVE

20 tests covering:
- ProgressBar creation and updates
- Rendering with various options
- All bar styles
- Spinner frame cycling
- Duration formatting
- Progress clamping (0.0-1.0)

---

### Overall Assessment: ✅ PASS

**Status**: Implemented ahead of schedule (Phase 2+ feature)
**Quality**: Well-designed with builder pattern and comprehensive tests
**Recommendation**: No changes needed; ready for use

Note: Since there's no formal spec, validation was done against implementation quality and Python Rich patterns.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-1","validation"]}

 {"id":"bd-xbu","title":"Validate Columns Layout Algorithm","description":"Validate Columns layout component against RICH_SPEC.md and Python Rich source.

COMPONENT PURPOSE:
Columns renders multiple renderables side-by-side with automatic width distribution.

VALIDATION CHECKLIST:
- [ ] Column width calculation algorithm
- [ ] Equal width distribution mode
- [ ] Ratio-based width distribution
- [ ] Expand mode (fill available width)
- [ ] Padding between columns
- [ ] Vertical alignment within columns (top/middle/bottom)
- [ ] Overflow handling when content exceeds column width
- [ ] Empty column handling
- [ ] Single column edge case
- [ ] Mixed-height content alignment

KEY ALGORITHMS:
1. Available width = console_width - (padding * (num_columns - 1))
2. Per-column width = available_width / num_columns (equal mode)
3. Per-column width = available_width * ratio / total_ratio (ratio mode)

FILES TO EXAMINE:
- src/renderables/columns.rs
- Python rich/columns.py for reference","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:11:01.838957632Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T19:47:15.704092515Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T19:47:15.704092515Z","close_reason":"Columns validation complete: 23 tests covering width calculation, equal width, expand mode, padding/gutter, alignment, edge cases (zero width, narrow, many items, CJK characters)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["phase-1","validation"]}
 {"id":"bd-xfla","title":"[EPIC] Design: Input Length Limits for Interactive Prompts","description":"## Background & Motivation

The `src/interactive.rs` module provides `Prompt` for gathering user input. Currently, there are **no limits** on input length, which could lead to memory exhaustion if extremely long input is provided.

## Problem Statement

Current input reading in Prompt (lines 218-226):
```rust
pub fn ask(&self, console: &Console) -> Result<String, PromptError> {
    // ...
    let mut reader = stdin.lock();
    let mut raw_input = String::new();
    reader.read_line(&mut raw_input)?;  // No length limit!
    // ...
}
```

Similar patterns exist in:
- `PasswordPrompt::ask()` (line 533-541)
- `ConfirmPrompt::ask()` (line 697-705)

## Attack Vectors

1. **Memory Exhaustion**: Malicious input could consume all available memory
2. **Denial of Service**: Long computation for validation/processing
3. **Buffer Overflow** (theoretical): While Rust prevents true buffer overflows, extremely long strings can cause allocation failures

## Risk Assessment

- **Severity**: Low (requires local console access or piped input)
- **Likelihood**: Low (requires intentional attack)
- **Impact**: Medium (process crash, potential system instability)
- **Overall**: Low priority security hardening

## Design Goals

1. **Reasonable Defaults**: Set sensible max input lengths
2. **Configurability**: Allow users to override limits
3. **Clear Errors**: Provide informative error messages on limit exceeded
4. **Backward Compatibility**: Existing code should work unchanged

## Recommended Approach

### Option A: Builder Configuration (Recommended)

```rust
impl<T> Prompt<T> {
    /// Set maximum input length in bytes (default: 1MB)
    pub fn max_input_length(mut self, max: usize) -> Self {
        self.max_input_length = Some(max);
        self
    }
}

// Usage
let name = Prompt::new("Enter name")
    .max_input_length(256)
    .ask(&console)?;
```

### Option B: Global Configuration

```rust
// In Console or global config
const DEFAULT_MAX_INPUT: usize = 1_048_576; // 1MB
```

### Option C: Read with Limit

```rust
fn read_line_limited(reader: &mut impl BufRead, max: usize) -> io::Result<String> {
    let mut buf = String::new();
    let mut limited = reader.take(max as u64);
    limited.read_line(&mut buf)?;
    if buf.len() >= max {
        return Err(io::Error::new(
            io::ErrorKind::InvalidInput,
            "Input exceeds maximum length"
        ));
    }
    Ok(buf)
}
```

## Scope

Files requiring modification:
- src/interactive.rs: Prompt, PasswordPrompt, ConfirmPrompt
- Potentially add new PromptError variant: InputTooLong

## Success Criteria

- All prompt types support configurable max length
- Default limit is reasonable (suggest 1MB)
- Clear error when limit exceeded
- Tests verify limit enforcement
- Documentation updated","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:14:06.511022677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:42:42.627851806Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:42:42.627784280Z","close_reason":"All child tasks complete","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-xl5","title":"Add Integration Tests for Table Rendering","description":"## Objective
Integration tests verifying complete Table rendering pipeline.

## Test Cases Required

### Basic Tables
- [ ] Single row, single column
- [ ] Multiple rows, single column
- [ ] Single row, multiple columns
- [ ] Full table (multiple rows and columns)

### Table Features
- [ ] With title
- [ ] With caption
- [ ] With header row
- [ ] With footer row
- [ ] Row separators (show_lines=true)
- [ ] No edge borders (show_edge=false)

### Column Sizing
- [ ] Auto-sizing columns
- [ ] Fixed width columns
- [ ] Min/max width constraints
- [ ] Ratio-based expansion
- [ ] Table wider than content (expand=true)
- [ ] Table narrower than content (collapse)

### Cell Features
- [ ] Multi-line cells
- [ ] Styled cell content
- [ ] Vertical alignment (top, middle, bottom)
- [ ] Horizontal alignment (left, center, right)

### Box Styles
- [ ] ASCII box
- [ ] ROUNDED box
- [ ] DOUBLE box
- [ ] HEAVY box
- [ ] safe_box fallback

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Empty table
- [ ] Empty cells
- [ ] Very narrow width
- [ ] Very wide content
- [ ] Wide characters in cells

## Files to Create/Modify
- tests/table_integration.rs (new file)","acceptance_criteria":"All table features tested. Rendered output matches expected.","notes":"## Logging Requirements

Table integration tests must have DETAILED logging for debugging:

1. **Table structure**: Columns, rows, options
2. **Width calculation**: 
   - Available width
   - Minimum widths per column
   - Maximum widths per column
   - Calculated widths
   - Collapse/expand operations
3. **Row rendering**: Content per cell, alignment applied
4. **Border drawing**: Characters used, positions

This is critical because table layout bugs are hard to diagnose without logs.

Example:
```rust
#[test]
fn test_table_width_calculation() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    let span = info_span!("table_width_calc", width = 50);
    let _enter = span.enter();
    
    let table = Table::new()
        .add_column(Column::new("A").width(20))
        .add_column(Column::new("B"))
        .add_column(Column::new("C"));
    
    debug!(columns = ?table.columns, "Table columns");
    
    let widths = table.calculate_widths(50);
    debug!(result_widths = ?widths, "Calculated widths");
    
    let total: usize = widths.iter().sum();
    info!(total_width = total, available = 50, "Width distribution");
    
    assert!(total <= 50, "Total width exceeds available");
}
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:24:12.280758298Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T06:53:36.563572723Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T06:53:36.563572723Z","close_reason":"tests/e2e_table.rs already contains 37 comprehensive integration tests covering all required table features: basic tables, column sizing, cell features, box styles, and edge cases.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-xl5","depends_on_id":"bd-33q","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:11Z","created_by":"import"}]}
